title: Transactions, Isolation, Concurrency, and Recovery · Relational Lens: Recovery Backup Restore
summary: Two operators reassign and resolve the same ticket while a failure occurs between related writes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql recovery backup restore increment in a concurrency-safe ticket transition protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration.
Compare locking, multiversion, optimistic, and serialization-retry strategies from workload evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Recovery Backup Restore · Theory Recovery Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
theory case — Operators A and B read ticket 103 as open; A assigns it while B resolves it and a failure occurs between event insert and status update. Identify the anomaly, transaction boundary, retry precondition, and restore evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Recovery Backup Restore contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Recovery Backup Restore · Theory Recovery Backup Restore.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration. For Predict Recovery Backup Restore · Theory Recovery Backup Restore, keep Recovery Backup Restore connected to executable relational evidence.
